data.title: As a non-technical user, I can run architect-foundation with --auto to skip engineering questions and get recommended defaults
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Add an --auto flag that reads project-understanding.md, selects recommended defaults (the "what do you recommend?" answers), and presents a single summary before one confirmation
**Why**: Non-technical users cannot meaningfully answer enforcement preference questions
**Scope**:
- IN: --auto flag parsing, read project-understanding.md for stack/architecture signals, select recommended defaults, present summary, diffs always shown for existing files before confirmation, single confirmation writes all
- OUT: Defining exact default values (implementation detail), re-run merge logic (separate story)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, architect-foundation accepts --auto and selects recommended defaults without prompting for engineering questions
  ← Satisfies: "accepts an --auto flag; when set, the skill reads project-understanding.md to infer stack and architecture signals and selects recommended defaults"
- When this story is complete, diffs against existing files are always shown before the single confirmation prompt in --auto mode
  ← Satisfies: "diffs against existing files are always shown before the single confirmation prompt"
- Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Reliability] --auto must handle stacks with no defined defaults (unknown/rust/golang) — current skill prompts user for these

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
